title: hieracium vulgatum subsp. subampliatum
intro: Hieracium vulgatum subsp. subampliatum, also known as spotted hawkweed or hawkweed, is a subspecies of perennial herb belonging to the dandelion family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Hieracium vulgatum subsp. subampliatum include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, as well as exhibiting potential antioxidant and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying or making infusions of its leaves, which can be used as a topical treatment or ingested in tea or capsule form.
